channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Woodside Assumes Operatorship of Aussie Gas Assets from ExxonMobil
item.description: The partners continue to each own 50 percent in the Gippsland Basin Joint Venture, which supplies about 40 percent of gas needs on Australia's east coast, and 32.5 percent in the Kipper Unit JV, Woodside said.

item.title: ACCC orders phase two review of Saipem-Subsea7 merger
item.description: The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) is set to conduct a detailed Phase 2 review of the proposed merger between Saipem and Subsea7.

item.title: Canada and Alberta advance review of west coast oil pipeline project
item.description: The Government of Canada has referred Alberta's proposed west coast oil pipeline project to the Major Projects Office (MPO) for evaluation as a potential national interest initiative.

item.title: ExxonMobil, QatarEnergy Affirm Commerciality of Cyprus Discoveries
item.description: QatarEnergy and ExxonMobil signed a 'commercial discovery declaration' with the Cypriot government for two discoveries in Block 10 in the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Oil Remains in a Divided Market
item.description: Oil remains in a divided market, Waleed Said, Technical Analyst at GivTrade, stated.

item.title: Soaring Imports Push India’s Crude Stocks to Near 1-Year High
item.description: India’s strategic and commercial crude oil inventories have jumped to a nearly one-year high as the world’s third-largest crude oil buyer boosted its imports to a record high in June. As at the end of June, India’s…

item.title: TotalEnergies Offers Millions of Iraqi Barrels to Asia
item.description: TotalEnergies is offering millions of barrels of Iraqi crude that can be promptly delivered to Asia, according to traders, adding to a market already brimming with supply.

item.title: TotalEnergies sells 8.5% stake in Marjoram gas field to Inpex
item.description: TotalEnergies has announced the sale of its 8.5% net interest in the Marjoram gas field, located in Block 2E offshore Malaysia, to Inpex for $350m.

item.title: Citi: Oil Could Sink to $60 as Hormuz Traffic Normalizes
item.description: Brent Crude prices could plunge to as low as $60 per barrel by the end of the year, says Citigroup, which expects flows through the Strait of Hormuz to soon normalize and the U.S. and Iran to reach a deal in the coming…

item.title: India Denies Directly Exporting Fuel to Russia, But Admits Traders May Be
item.description: Indian refiners are not directly exporting any refined petroleum products to fuel-starved Russia, although some supplies from traders are likely reaching Russia, India’s Oil Minister, Hardeep Singh Puri, has said…

item.title: Oil Set for Fourth Straight Weekly Loss as Hormuz Flows Return
item.description: Oil futures were on track to post their fourth consecutive weekly loss early on Friday as the tentative reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the uptick in oil flows weigh down prices. In Asian trade early on Friday…

item.title: PJM on Maximum Alert to Prevent Outages Amid Extreme Heat Wave
item.description: PJM Interconnection, the operator of the largest U.S. electric grid, has issued a maximum generation alert for July 3 asking power producers to maximize electricity generation amid soaring demand in the current extreme…

item.title: Japan's LNG Imports Fall 7% as Utilities Chase Cheaper Coal
item.description: Japan last month reduced gas-fired generation in favor of coal as the price of liquefied natural gas remained elevated despite multiple reports of energy flows out of the Strait of Hormuz improving. The country…

item.title: Alberta and Ottawa Greenlight New Pacific Pipeline
item.description: Alberta’s Premier Danielle Smith and federal Prime Minister Mark Carney have made the official announcement of a new oil pipeline, to be built to Canada’s Pacific coast, broadening the access of Canadian oil producers…

item.title: India Coal Power Generation Jumps 14% in June
item.description: India's coal-powered electricity generation last month surged by 14% on the year to 120.20 billion kWh as drier, hotter weather prompted a significant increase in demand. Reuters noted in a report on the news that this…

item.title: Saudi Oil Exports Approach Pre-War Levels
item.description: Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports have surged close to their pre-war levels as the kingdom starts getting its tankers through the Strait of Hormuz again.

item.title: Oil Holds Near Prewar Lows
item.description: Oil prices steadied as rising exports through Hormuz reinforced concerns about a growing global supply surplus.

item.title: UAE Rewrites Offshore Oil Pricing To Capture Asian Markets
item.description: Previously, we reported that Abu Dhabi’s flagship crude, Murban crude, has rapidly risen to prominence, with Murban crude futures having rapidly evolved from a regional benchmark into a primary global pricing standard…

item.title: Tehran Is Racing the Clock to Export as Much Oil as Possible
item.description: Iran is wasting no time in returning full steam to the oil export market after the Islamic Republic and the United States agreed in mid-June on a 60-day window to negotiate a deal. Since the memorandum of understanding…

item.title: Europe’s Energy Transition Stalls as Heat Wave Exposes the Cost of Inaction
item.description: London Climate Action Week took place during an intense heat wave last week, causing some of the planned events to be cancelled and calling extra attention to the urgency of the summit’s cause. One of the biggest…
